A regression test for issue 640 in the JerryScript engine.

# Purpose
This code defines a simple JavaScript function named [`f_0`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry/regression-test-issue-640f_0>). Inside the function, a local variable also named [`f_0`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry/regression-test-issue-640f_0>) is declared and assigned the value `10`. The function does not return any value or perform any operations beyond this variable declaration. This code demonstrates the concept of variable shadowing, where a local variable within a function has the same name as the function itself, effectively hiding the function's name within its own scope. The functionality is narrow, focusing on illustrating this specific behavior in JavaScript.

    - Assign the value 10 to the local variable `f_0`.
- **Output**: No output is produced.
